title: acer monspessulanum subsp. microphyllum
intro: Acer monspessulanum subsp. microphyllum, also known as the dwarf Montpellier maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Acer genus. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits associated with this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. Acer monspessulanum subsp. microphyllum can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
